textPlain: I’m really curious how much it costs to inject information like this into an LLM as people say training an LLM is very expensive, so if you want a domain specific LLM, how much does the additional training cost to get this?

I wish the authors calculated a plot of model size (number of params) vs number of triples it can hold before the memory collapse happens.It's hard to map the frequency of knowledge injection to a real world understanding of "how much knowledge" can a 4B param model hold?
I wonder if this depends on what is inside the domain specific data.I’m happy to see ML papers on hacker news.

It sounds like you're talking about fine-tuning an existing model. That's not what this paper did - they studied the effect of training small models entirely from scratch with varying amounts of domain knowledge.I still haven't seen strong evidence that fine-tuning to add extra knowledge is effective, but I'd be delighted to learn otherwise.
There is a study that gives a rule of thumb of ~2 bits per param for a model's memorization capacity: https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.05405
